questions: Should external media be eagerly metadata-resolved for every detected source URL before the agent prompt, or is exposing stable external refs and resolving only refs selected for publishing acceptable? I assume lazy publish-time resolution to avoid downloading irrelevant links.

Where should durable downloaded media live in production: local disk cache, Supabase storage, or both? I assume local cache path plus DB metadata is acceptable unless the deployment uses ephemeral disks.
What exact max download size and timeout should be enforced? I assume conservative defaults such as 25 MB and 15 seconds total unless existing production limits say otherwise.

Should YouTube/Vimeo/Twitch links be downloadable for TopicEditor, or only treated as fallback links? They appear in the curator safelist, but attaching long-form video files to Discord may exceed practical limits.

success_criteria.criterion: Existing attachment and embed media refs normalize, validate, render, and publish exactly as before for current tests.
`normalize_media_ref` accepts `kind: "external"` refs and rejects malformed or out-of-range external refs with deterministic errors or rejected tool transitions.

Source message payloads and `get_message_context` expose compact `media_refs_available` entries for safelisted external URLs found in message content and embeds, with Discord attachment/embed refs listed first.
External URL extraction is restricted to the safelist and enforces timeout, max-size, content-type, and sanitized logging behavior in unit-tested resolver paths.
yt-dlp is invoked in metadata-only mode before any download attempt, and tests prove metadata failures fall back without raising into TopicEditor publishing.
Resolved external media is cached by stable URL key plus content hash so repeated publish attempts can reuse cache without re-downloading.
Structured publishing interleaves selected external media with block text; on resolver/download/file-send failure it posts the original link and continues publishing remaining text/units.
No code path reintroduces direct LiveTopCreations-style auto-posting at the reaction threshold; reaction-qualified media remains an auto-shortlist-to-watching flow.
Logs and trace metadata describe resolved/downloaded/skipped/fallback outcomes without emitting query tokens or secrets from URLs.
New resolver code keeps pure parsing/normalization helpers separate from subprocess, network, filesystem, and DB side effects.
Tests avoid real network calls, real yt-dlp execution, and real Discord sends by using stubs/fakes.
A manual production smoke test can publish a structured topic containing a Reddit or X external media ref and observe either an attached file or fallback link in the expected block position.

assumptions: External media resolution should be lazy for selected block media_refs, not eager for every link in the source window.
The safelist should start from the curator list and add canonical Reddit/Twitter/X/Instagram domains required by this task.
The resolver may use local filesystem cache plus DB metadata unless production storage requirements say otherwise.
External resolver failures are normal outcomes, not exceptional TopicEditor failures.
Structured block publishing is the target path; legacy simple-topic publishing does not need new external media binding.
Tests should use fakes for subprocess, HTTP, cache, and Discord file sends.
